The Senate reconciliation bill, known as the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA), extends major components of the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (TCJA) using a 'current policy' baseline, as reported by credible sources such as The Hill, Tax Foundation, and Penn Wharton Budget Model. The bill was signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025.

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) was signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025. This law overhauls the clean energy tax credit framework established by the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA). Specifically, the OBBBA introduces accelerated repeal schedules for many renewable energy credits, shortens eligibility deadlines, tightens domestic content rules, and eliminates several electric-vehicle and residential energy incentives. The domestic content requirements are made mandatory for obtaining the IRA's Production Tax Credits (45Y) for projects beginning construction on or after June 16, 2025.

The domestic content requirements for obtaining the IRA's Investment Tax Credits (48E) became mandatory as part of the 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act' signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025. This act modified the existing requirements, specifying domestic content percentages for qualified facilities and energy storage technology, effective for projects starting construction after certain dates.

The House of Representatives passed a reconciliation bill (H.R. 1) on May 22, 2025, which amends the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) and introduces Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC) restrictions for the Advanced Manufacturing Production Tax Credits (45X). These restrictions disallow credits to taxpayers who are 'specified foreign entities' or 'foreign-influenced entities' and require material produced or assembled without assistance from prohibited foreign entities.
